lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know of anything I can do in Morytania?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well... I don't think so. There's not much to do in that place.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, not a great deal... but there is something you could do for me if you're interested. Though it is quite dangerous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, what is it, I may be able to help?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: There's a man called Filliman who lives in Mort Myre. He comes by the temple from time to time but I haven't seen him recently. I was wondering if you could check in on him?

lines.speaker: Drezen
lines.text: I must warn you though, it could be dangerous. Mort Myre is filled with ghasts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll go look for him.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: That's great! Many thanks!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Now, please be aware of the ghasts, you cannot attack them, only Filliman knew how to take them on. Just run from them if you can. If you get lost, try to make your way back to the temple.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Please take this food to Filliman, he'll likely appreciate it. His home is somewhere in the southern end of Mort Myre. I don't know the exact location I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, if he's there and he's still alive, I'll find him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who exactly is this Filliman?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Filliman Tarlock is his full name and he's a druid. He lives in Mort Myre much like a hermit, but there's many a traveller who he's helped.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Most people that come this way tell stories of when they were lost and paths that just seemed to 'open up' before them! I think it was Filliman Tarlock helping out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's Mort Myre?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Mort Myre is a decayed and dangerous swamp to the south east of here. Legend says it was once a beautiful forest called Humblethorn. Now, it's filled with vile emanations from within Morytania.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The swamp decays everything. We put a fence around it to stop unwary travellers going in. Anyone who dies in the swamp is forever cursed to haunt it as a ghast.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ghasts attack travellers, turning food to rotten filth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a ghast?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: A Ghast is a poor soul who died in Mort Myre. They're undead of a special class and they're untouchable as far as I'm aware! Filliman knows how to tackle them though.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: When they attack, ghasts will devour any food you have. If you have no food, they'll draw their nourishment from you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I don't think I can help.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: That's fine, I'm sure someone else will be along shortly who can. Now, if you excuse me, I do have some things to be getting on with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, not interested.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, I understand. You must be busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Have you managed to find Filliman yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No not yet.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Please go and look for him, I would appreciate it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where should I look for Filliman Tarlock again?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Search to the south of Mort Myre. Remember that he's a druid so he can conceal himself within nature quite well. My guess is that he lives in the southern area of the swamp, though he could be anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Explain to me what a Ghast is again.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: A Ghast is a poor soul who died in Mort Myre. They're undead of a special class, they're untouchable apart from with special druidic items.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Filliman knew how to tackle them, but I've not heard from him in a long time. Ghasts, when they attack, will devour any food you have. If you have no food, they'll draw their nourishment from you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the story with Mort Myre?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Mort Myre was once a beautiful forest by the name of Humblethorn until the evil denizens of Morytania descended. Now their evil emanations have putrified and diseased the forest into a decaying swamp of death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the story with Filliman Tarlock?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Filliman is a druid of some considerable power. He helped many people in Morytania escape when the evil descended upon the land. His knowledge of plants and nature was exceptional.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: But one day, he was betrayed by some of the people who he had tried to help. This naturally made him more careful when dealing with strangers again and so, instead of showing himself, he would follow them.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: He would follow them at a distance and make the path clear for them, showing them to the temple and to salvation. Saradomin bless him! He is a good man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Many thanks to you, [player name]!

sections.title: Entering the gate to Mort Myre Swamp

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Cannot wake up... Where am I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? What's this?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: What did I write down now? Put it in the knot hole.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Ahhrs Oooohh arhhhhAHhhh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh! Now you're just not making any sense at all! I just cannot understand you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Oh, I understand you! At last, someone who doesn't just mumble. I understand what you're saying!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm wearing an amulet of ghost speak!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Why you poor fellow, have you passed away and you want to send a message back to a loved one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err.. Not exactly...

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: You have come to haunt my dreams until I pass on your message to a dearly loved one. I understand. Pray, tell me who would you like me to pass a message on to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ermm, you don't understand... It's just that..

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, please don't be upset or anything... But you're the ghost!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Don't be silly now! That in no way reflects the truth!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long have you been a ghost?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: What?! Don't be preposterous! I'm not a ghost! How could you say something like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it's true, you're a ghost... well, at least that is to say, you're sort of not alive anymore.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Don't be silly, I can see you, I can see that tree. If I were dead, I wouldn't be able to see anything.. What you say just doesn't reflect the truth. You'll have to try harder to pull one over on me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like being a ghost?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Oh, it's quite.... Oh... Trying to catch me out were you! Anyone can clearly see that I am not a ghost!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you are a ghost, look at yourself! I can see straight through you! You're as dead as this swamp! Err... No offence or anything...

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: No I won't take offence because I'm not dead and I'm afraid you'll have to come up with some pretty conclusive proof before I believe it. What a strange dream this is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Ahhrs Oooohh arhhhhAHhhh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Oh, hello there, do you still think I'm dead? It's hard to see how I could be dead when I'm still in the world. I can see everything quite clearly. And nothing of what you say reflects the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do think you're dead and I'll prove it somehow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here take a look at this, perhaps you can see that you're utterly transparent now!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Well, that is the most peculiar thing I've ever experienced. This mirror must somehow be dysfunctional. Strange how well it reflects the stagnant swamp behind me, but there is nothing of my own

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: visage apparent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's because you're dead! Dead as a door nail.. Deader in fact... You bear a remarkable resemblance to worm bait! Err.. No offence...

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: I think you might be right my friend, though I still feel very much alive. It is strange how I still come to be here and yet I've not turned into a Ghast.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: It must be a sign... Yes a sign... I must try to find out what it means. Now, where did I put my journal?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Ahhrs Oooohh arhhhhAHhhh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, I found this, maybe you can use it?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: My journal! That should help to collect my thoughts.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: It's all coming back to me now. It looks like I came to a violent and bitter end but that's not important now. I just have to figure out what I am going to do now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Being dead, what options do you think you have? I'm not trying to be rude or something, but it's not like you have many options is it? I mean, it's either up or down for you isn't it?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Hmm, well you're a poetic one aren't you. Your material world logic stands you in good stead... If you're standing in the material world...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what's your plan?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: In my former incarnation I was Filliman Tarlock, a great druid of some power. I spent many years in this place, which was once a forest and I would wish to protect it as a nature sprit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Won't you help me to become a nature spirit? I could really use your help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Will you help me to become a nature spirit? The directions for becoming one are a bit vague, I need three things but I know how to get one of them. Perhaps you can help collect the rest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might be interested, what's involved?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Well, the book says, that I need, and I quote:- 'Something with faith', 'something from nature' and 'something of the 'spirit-to-become' freely given'. Hmm, I know how to get something from nature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that does seem a bit vague.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Hmm, it does and I could understand if you didn't want to help. However, if you could perhaps at least get the item from nature, that would be a start. Perhaps we can figure out the rest as we go along.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: This spell needs to be cast in the swamp after you have been blessed. I'm afraid you'll need to go to the temple to the North and ask a member of the clergy to bless you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Blessed, what does that do?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: It is required if you're to cast this druid spell. Once you've cast the spell, you should find something from nature. Bring it back to me and then we'll try to figure out the other things we need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Filliman again after using a mirror on him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again..

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Oh, hello... Sorry, you've caught me at a bad time, it's just that I've had a sign you see and I need to find my journal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you put it?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Well, if I knew that, I wouldn't still be looking for it. However, I do remember something about a knot? Perhaps I was meant to tie a knot or something?

sections.title: Using a mirror on Filliman again

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Yes, I know, I'm already dead! Thanks for reminding me.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Ahhrs Oooohh arhhhhAHhhh.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Thanks for the journal, I've been reading it. It looks like I came to a violent and bitter end but that's not really important. I just have to figure out what I am going to do now?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Hello there, have you been blessed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Well, hurry up!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: How's life been treating you since you got blessed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not so bad!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: It's funny, because when I look at you, there does seem to be something of the faith about you. Have you considered a life of service to Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I serve Saradomin in other ways.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Fair enough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again! I'm helping Filliman, he plans to become a nature spirit. I have a spell to cast but first I need to be blessed. Can you bless me?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: But you haven't sneezed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're so funny!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But can you bless me?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Very well my friend, prepare yourself for the blessings of Saradomin. Here we go!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ashustru, blessidium, adverturasi, fidum!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: There you go my friend, you're now blessed. It's funny, now I look at you, there seems to be something of the faith about you. Anyway, good luck with your quest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Many thanks!

sections.title: Finding and using the three items on the stones

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Aha, yes, that seems right well done!

sections.title: Talking to Filliman again on the orange stone

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've solved the puzzle!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Oh really.. Have you placed all the items on the stones? Ok, well, lets try! ~ The druid attempts to cast a spell.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Hmm, something still doesn't seem right. I think we need something more before we can continue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've solved the puzzle!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Oh really.. Have you placed all the items on the stones? Ok, well, lets try! ~ The druid attempts to cast a spell.~

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Aha, everything seems to be in place! You can come through now into the grotto for the final section of my transformation.

sections.title: Searching all the stones after the spell
Talking to Filliman outside the grove again

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Please come down into the grotto, we have much to discuss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I've been blessed but I don't know what to do now.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Well, you need to bring 'something from nature', 'something with faith' and 'something of the spirit-to-become freely given.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, but what does that mean?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Hmm, it is a conundrum, however, if you use that Bloom spell I gave you, you should be able to get something from nature. Once you have that, we may be able to puzzle the rest out.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Did you manage to get something from nature?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Wonderful, the mushroom represents 'something from nature'. Now we need to work out what the other components of the spell are!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: The three things are: 'Something with faith', 'something from nature' and 'something of the spirit-to-become freely given'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, and 'something from nature' is the mushroom from the bloom spell you gave me?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Yes, that's correct, that seems right to me. The other things we need are 'something with faith' and 'something of the spirit-to-become freely given.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any ideas what those things are?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: I'm sorry my friend, but I do not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should we do when we have those things?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Ah yes, I looked this up. It says,.. 'to arrange upon three rocks around the spirit-to-become...'. Then I must cast a spell. As you can see, I've already placed the rocks. I must have planned to do this before I died!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we just place the components on any rock?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Well, the only thing the journal says is that 'something with faith stands south of the spirit-to-become', but I'm so confused now I don't really know what that means. Oh, if only I had all my faculties!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've solved the puzzle!

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Oh really.. Have you placed all the items on the stones? Ok, well, lets try! ~ The druid attempts to cast a spell.~

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Hmm, something still doesn't seem right. I think we need something more before we can continue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have another bloom scroll please?

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: I would my friend, but you don't seem to have enough space in your inventory.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Sure, but please look after this one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Filliman inside his grove

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: Well, hello there again, I was just enjoying the grotto. Many thanks for your help, I couldn't have become a Spirit of nature without you.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: I must complete the transformation now. Just stand there and watch the show, apparently it's quite good!

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Hmmm, good, the transformation is complete. Now, my friend, in return for your assistance, I will help you to kill the Ghasts. First bring to me a silver sickle so that I can bless it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A silver sickle? What's that?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: The sickle is the symbol and weapon of the Druid, you need to construct one of silver so that I can bless it, with its powers you will be able to defeat the Ghasts of Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I get a silver sickle?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: You could make one yourself if you're artisan enough. I've heard of a distant sandy place where you can buy the mould that you require, it's similar in many respects to the creating of a holy symbol.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do to the silver sickle?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Why, I will give it my blessings so that the very swamp in which you stand will blossom and bloom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can a blessed sickle help me to defeat the Ghasts?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: My blessings will entice nature to bloom in Mort Myre! And then with natures harvest you can fill a druids pouch and release the Ghasts from their torment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Filliman inside his grotto again

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Have you brought me the silver sickle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sorry, not yet!

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Well, come to me when you have it.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Have you brought me the silver sickle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here it is. What are you going to do with it?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: My friend, I will bless it for you and you will then be able to accomplish great things. Now then, I must cast the enchantment. You can bless a new sickle by dipping it in the holy water of the grotto.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Now you can go forth and make the swamp bloom. Collect natures bounty to fill a druids pouch. So armed will the Ghasts be bound to you until, you flee or they are defeated.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Before I can make this grotto into an Altar of Nature, I need to be sure that the Ghasts will be kept at bay. Go forth into Mort Myre and slay three Ghasts. You'll be releasing their souls from Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Filliman Tarlock
lines.text: I would give you the druid pouch, but you don't seem to have enough space in your inventory.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: You'll need this in order to collect together natures bounty. When it contains items, it will bind the Ghast to you until you flee or it is defeated.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Hello again my friend, have you defeated three Ghasts as I asked you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Well, when you do, please come to me and I'll reward you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get to attack the Ghasts?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Go forth and with the sickle make the swamp bloom. Collect natures bounty to fill a druids pouch. So armed will the Ghasts be bound to you until, you flee or they are defeated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this pouch for?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: It is for collecting natures bounty, once it contains the blossomed items of the swamp, it will make the Ghasts appear and you can then attack them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do with this sickle?

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: You may use it wisely within the area of Mort Myre to affect natures balance and bring forth a bounty of natures harvest. Once collected into the druid pouch will the Ghast be apparent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost my sickle!

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: If you should lose the blessed sickle, simply bring another to my altar of nature and refresh it in the grotto waters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks.

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Hello again my friend, have you defeated three Ghasts as I asked you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've killed all three and their spirits have been released!

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Many thanks my friend, you have completed your quest! I can now change this place into a holy sanctuary! And forever will it now be an Altar of Nature!

lines.speaker: Nature Spirit
lines.text: Welcome to my Altar to Nature! Farewell my friend, and keep those Ghasts at bay!
